zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert de la Mare於1314年出生在英國維爾爾拉維頓時，他的父親Sir Peter de la Mare20歲，母親Joan Achard21歲。他與Maud De Hastings結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1382年1月13日在英國英格蘭維爾爾拉維頓享年68歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Hung, Cuong, Anh, Thanh, Ha, Hoang, Tuan, Chau, Hao, Hien, Hoa, Hue, Nam, Phong, Sinh, Tuong, Min, Tam, Tanh, Thai, Tham, Tinh, Yeong.  Chinese Hong, Hang, Sang, Tong, Chan, Chi, Hwa Ja, Hyung, Kam, Ki Bong, Kin, Man.
Vietnamese: from the Chinese surname 羅, see   Luo   1.
Vietnamese (Lã): from the Chinese surname 呂, see Lu 6 and 3 below.

variants.name: Luo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jian, Yi, Ping, Yong, Zhen, Feng, Hong, Ming, Jin, Bin, Chen, Hai, Lan, Min, Huan, Hung, Shen, Tian.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 羅, meaning ‘net for catching birds’ or ‘to collect’ in Chinese: (i) from Luo (羅), the name of an ancient state (located in present-day Hubei province). After it was annexed by the state of Chu during the early Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), people from the state migrated southwards and adopted Luo (羅) as their surname. (ii) from the Po Duo Luo (破多羅) and Chi Luo (叱羅) families from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China, who changed their original surnames to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) from the personal name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅), leader of an ancient Turkic tribe who are believed to have migrated to China during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ). His descendants inherited his name Hu Se Luo (斛瑟羅) as their surname and shortened it to 羅 during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iv) from the name of one of the seven families from the Bandun Man, an ethnic group residing in ancient southwestern China (located mainly in present-day Sichuan province).

nameMeanings.name: Mare
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Americo, Cesareo, Fernando, Ines, Javier, Jose, Marcos.  Portuguese Henrique.  Italian Antonio, Gino, Olindo, Silvio, Vito.  Dutch Andries.
Italian: topographic name for someone living by the sea, from mare ‘sea’.
English: variant of   Mear  .

variants.name: Marucci
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Antonio, Silvio, Angelo, Carmela, Domenic, Donato, Egidio, Guido, Licinio, Lorenzo, Luciano.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of the personal name Maruccio, a pet form of Mare or Mari .

variants.name: Marelli
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Giuseppe, Angelo, Dante.
Italian:
patronymic or plural form of a diminutive of   Mare  .

variants.name: Mear
meanings: Mear, a field; also from mear or mearh, a horse
Anglo-Saxon: Mere, lake, pool, sea

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert de la Mare was born in 1314, in Lavington, Wiltshire, England, his father, Sir Peter de la Mare, was 20 and his mother, Joan Achard, was 21. He married Maud De Hastings. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 13 January 1382, in Lavington, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 68.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert de la Mare est né en 1314, à Lavington, Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter de la Mare, avait 20 ans et sa mère, Joan Achard, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Maud De Hastings. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 13 janvier 1382, à Lavington, Wiltshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 68 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Robert de la Mare 1314 in Lavington, Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peter de la Mare 20 und seine Mutter Joan Achard 21. Er hat Maud De Hastings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 13. Januar 1382 im Alter von 68 Jahren in Lavington, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de la Mare è nato il nel 1314 nel Lavington, Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Peter de la Mare, aveva 20 anni e il suo madre, Joan Achard, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Maud De Hastings. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 13 gennaio 1382, nel Lavington, Wiltshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 68 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert de la Mare(이)가 1314년 영국 윌츠히르 래빙턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter de la Mare(은)는 20세였고, 그의 어머니 Joan Achard(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Maud De Hastings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1382년 1월 13일 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 래빙턴에서 68세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert de la Mare nasceu em 1314, em Lavington, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter de la Mare, tinha 20 anos e sua mãe, Joan Achard, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud De Hastings. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 13 de janeiro de 1382, em Lavington, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 68 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert de la Mare родился в 1314 г. в местности лавингтон, уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Peter de la Mare было 20 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Achard, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud De Hastings. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 13 января 1382 г. в местности лавингтон, уилцхир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 68 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Robert de la Mare nació en 1314, en Lavington, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter de la Mare, tenía 20 años y su mamá, Joan Achard, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Maud De Hastings. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 13 de enero de 1382, en Lavington, Wiltshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 68 años.
